variables.content: Extrapolating the finite-size DMC effective-mass data to the thermodynamic limit using the single-power-law scaling with exponent \\(\\gamma=3/2\\) yields the following quasiparticle effective masses \\(m^{*}(\\infty)\\) expressed in Hartree atomic units (a.u.): for the paramagnetic (unpolarized) 2D HEG, \\(m^{*}(r_{s}=1)=0.955(2)\\), \\(m^{*}(r_{s}=5)=1.04(2)\\), and \\(m^{*}(r_{s}=10)=1.03(4)\\); for the fully spin-polarized (ferromagnetic) 2D HEG, \\(m^{*}(r_{s}=1)=0.851(5)\\), \\(m^{*}(r_{s}=5)=0.74(1)\\), and \\(m^{*}(r_{s}=10)=0.70(3)\\). These numerical values are the principal thermodynamic-limit benchmarks reported for clean, continuum-model 2D HEGs in this work.

The quasiparticle effective masses m^{*} reported in the literature from DMC fits to the single-particle energy band and the ground-state total-energy parametrizations reported in the literature as continuous functions E_{\mathrm{corr}}(r_s,\zeta) of density parameter r_s and spin polarization \zeta are mutually consistent within their stated uncertainties and can be combined algebraically via standard Fermi-liquid thermodynamic relations (for example F_1^{s} = m^{*}-1 for the paramagnetic HEG and evaluation of F_0^{s,a} from derivatives of E(r_s,\zeta)) to produce numerical estimates of the dimensionless Fermi liquid parameters F_l for the 2D HEG.
      Refs. 11,23,24

Because direct thermodynamic-limit extrapolation of the discrete-angle DMC-determined Fermi liquid parameters proved unreliable, a set of “best available” numerical recommendations for the Fermi liquid parameters was assembled by combining independently published QMC results for related quantities: quasiparticle effective masses $m^{*}$ obtained from DMC energy-band calculations and smooth QMC-based parameterizations of the ground-state correlation energy.  Using the literature effective masses together with compressibility and spin-susceptibility values derived from the total-energy parameterizations and the Landau relations yields recommended values such as, for the paramagnetic 2D HEG: at $r_{s}=1$, $F_{0}^{s}=-0.495(2)$, $F_{0}^{a}=-0.346(2)$, $F_{1}^{s}=-0.053(3)$; at $r_{s}=5$ and $10$ analogous recommended values are provided; and for the ferromagnetic 2D HEG recommended values include at $r_{s}=1$, $F_{0}=-0.428(2)$ and $\bar{F}_1=-0.159(3)$.  These assembled values combine ground-state energy parameterizations and published effective masses to provide pragmatic numerical recommendations when direct thermodynamic-limit determinations of $F_l$ from discrete-angle DMC data are unreliable.

For the paramagnetic (unpolarized) 2D HEG over the density-parameter range \\(r_{s}=1\\) to 10, let \\(m^{*}(\\infty)\\) denote the quasiparticle effective mass extrapolated to the thermodynamic limit by fitting the finite-size DMC effective masses to the single-power-law form \\(m^{*}(N)=m^{*}(\\infty)+b\\,N^{-\\gamma}\\) and adopting the exponent \\(\\gamma=3/2\\) for the extrapolation. The proposition is: the extrapolated numerical values \\(m^{*}(r_{s}=1)=0.955(2)\\), \\(m^{*}(r_{s}=5)=1.04(2)\\), and \\(m^{*}(r_{s}=10)=1.03(4)\\) indicate that the true thermodynamic-limit quasiparticle effective mass of the clean continuum-model paramagnetic 2D HEG remains close to unity across this \\(r_{s}\\) range and that there is no significant enhancement of \\(m^{*}\\) at low density for this physical system class.

The proposition: For Fermi liquid parameters $F_l$ (or $F_l/m^{*}$) of the two-dimensional homogeneous electron gas (2D HEG) computed in square periodic simulation cells containing $N$ electrons, the leading systematic finite-size correction arising from long-range correlation effects scales asymptotically as $N^{\gamma}$ with exponent $\gamma=-1/4$ (i.e., a correction term proportional to $N^{-1/4}$), so that finite-$N$ results can be extrapolated to the thermodynamic limit by fitting to the form $F_l(N)=c+aN^{-1/4}$; this scaling is the same as that argued in the theoretical analyses of long-range-correlation finite-size effects (Holzmann et al.).

For a sequence of computed finite-size quasiparticle effective masses \\(m^{*}(N)\\) obtained by DMC for a 2D HEG at fixed density and spin polarization over the available range of system sizes \\(N\\), the proposition is: the dependence of \\(m^{*}(N)\\) on \\(N\\) can be modelled to good approximation by a single-term power-law finite-size correction,
      $$
      m^{*}(N)=m^{*}(\\infty)+b\\,N^{-\\gamma},
      $$
      with approximately constant parameters \\(m^{*}(\\infty)\\), \\(b\\), and exponent \\(\\gamma\\) across the sampled \\(N\\)-range, so that this parametric form provides a valid basis for extrapolation to the thermodynamic limit within the statistical precision of the DMC data.

For the two-dimensional homogeneous electron gas (2D HEG), the leading finite-size error in Fermi-liquid-derived quantities such as the angular-momentum Fermi liquid parameters F_l or the Fourier-projected quantities F_l/m^{*} behaves asymptotically as a power law in the finite-system particle number N with exponent -1/4; equivalently,
      \Delta F_l(N) \equiv F_l(N)-F_l(\infty) \propto N^{-1/4},
      so that fitting computed F_l(N) values to the form c + a N^{-1/4} yields an estimate of the thermodynamic-limit intercept c = F_l(\infty). This statement adopts the theoretical asymptotic exponent \gamma = -1/4 predicted for long-range-correlation-dominated finite-size errors.
      Refs. 13,14

The Fermi liquid parameters divided by the quasiparticle effective mass, $\;F_{l}^{s,a}/m^{*}$ (symmetric and antisymmetric channels for the paramagnetic HEG) and $F_{l}/m^{*}$ (ferromagnetic HEG), were obtained from the discrete-angle DMC interaction functions by first computing the Fourier components
      $$
      a_{l}^{\sigma\sigma'}=\frac{2}{\pi}\int_{0}^{\pi}N f_{\sigma\sigma'}(\theta)\cos(l\theta)\,d\theta,
      $$
      and then using the relations for an $N$-electron paramagnetic finite cell of density parameter $r_s$
      $$
      \frac{F_{l}^{s,a}}{m^{*}}=\frac{r_s^2}{4}\big[a_{l}^{\uparrow\uparrow}\pm a_{l}^{\uparrow\downarrow}\big],
      $$
      and for a ferromagnetic finite cell
      $$
      \frac{F_l}{m^{*}}=\frac{r_s^2}{4}a_{l}^{\uparrow\uparrow},
      $$
      where $m^{*}$ denotes the quasiparticle effective mass.  Numerical angular integration of the available nonuniform discrete angles $\{\theta_i\}$ was performed using a generalized composite Simpson's rule (piecewise quadratic interpolation adapted to a nonuniform grid), and the result was compared with a generalized composite trapezoidal rule to place an upper bound on the quadrature error.  Finite-size values of $F_l/m^{*}$ obtained at multiple closed-shell electron counts were extrapolated to the thermodynamic limit $N\to\infty$ by fitting the finite-$N$ data to the model $F_l(N)=c+aN^{\gamma}$ with the exponent fixed at $\gamma=-1/4$ (i.e., leading finite-size correction $\propto N^{-1/4}$) motivated by theoretical analyses of long-range-correlation contributions; ordinary (unweighted) least-squares fits were used because oscillatory shell-filling deviations of the finite-size data are larger than the nominal Monte Carlo error bars, and the quoted uncertainties on the extrapolated parameters reflect the scatter of the finite-$N$ data under these fits.

The conversion of the fitted linear coefficient $\gamma = 0.11\ \mathrm{J/K^2}$ to an effective quasiparticle mass $m^* = 3.2\,m_3$ (with $m_3$ the bare mass of a $^3$He atom) and to a Fermi temperature $T_F \approx 600\ \mathrm{mK}$ assumes that a second-layer $^3$He film at areal density $\rho=15.0\ \mathrm{nm}^{-2}$ behaves as an ideal, homogeneous two-dimensional Fermi liquid over $0.1\ \mathrm{mK}\lesssim T\lesssim 80\ \mathrm{mK}$ and that the standard low-temperature proportionality between the linear-in-$T$ heat-capacity coefficient $\gamma$ and the quasiparticle effective mass $m^*$ for a two-dimensional Fermi gas at that areal density holds without significant modification by substrate coupling, finite-size effects, strong vertex corrections, or anomalous non-Fermi-liquid contributions.
